name: markdown-grid-tables

metadata.name: markdown-grid-tables
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: Python-Markdown extension to support grid tables.

documentation:
# Markdown Grid Tables

[Python-Markdown](https://python-markdown.github.io/) [extension](https://python-markdown.github.io/extensions/api/) to support grid tables.

[Fork of Markdown-GridTables by Alexander Abbott](https://github.com/smartboyathome/Markdown-GridTables)

modules.name: markdown_grid_tables._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: markdown_grid_tables
documentation:
Grid tables extension for Python-Markdown Add parsing of grid tables to Python-Markdown. These differ from simple tables in that they can contain multi-lined text and (in my opinion) are cleaner looking than simpe tables. They were inspired by reStructuredText's grid table syntax. This extension was loosely based on the 'table' extension for Python-Markdown by Waylan Limberg. An example of a grid table: +---------------+---------------+-----------------+ | First Header | Second Header | Third Header | +===============+===============+=================+ | A cell that | A cell that spans multiple | | spans | columns. | | multiple rows +---------------+-----------------+ | | One, two cell | Red & blue cell | +---------------+---------------+-----------------+ This should be generated as (but colspans and rowspans may not work at the moment):
First Header Second Header Third Header
A cell that spans multiple rows A cell that spans multiple columns
One, two cell Red & blue cell
Licensed under GPLv3 by Alexander Abbott. Links referenced during creation of this plugin: https://gist.github.com/1855764 http://packages.python.org/Markdown/extensions/api.html https://github.com/waylan/Python-Markdown/blob/master/markdown/extensions/tables.py http://docutils.sourceforge.net/docs/ref/rst/restructuredtext.html#grid-tables http://docutils.svn.sourceforge.net/viewvc/docutils/trunk/docutils/docutils/parsers/rst/tableparser.py?revision=7320&content-type=text%2Fplain

classes.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.start_row
documentation: Starting row for the cell.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.start_row
documentation:
Sets the starting row of the cell to either 0 or the value passed in,
depending on which is larger.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.start_col
documentation: Starting column for the cell.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.start_col
documentation:
Sets the starting column of the cell to either 0 or the value passed
in, depending on which is larger.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.width
documentation: Width (in number of characters) of the cell.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.width
documentation:
Sets the width (in number of characters) of the cell to either 1 or the
value passed in, depending on which is larger.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.height
documentation: Height (in number of characters) of the cell.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.height
documentation:
Sets the height (in number of characters) of the cell to either 1 or
the value passed in, depending on which is larger.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.colspan
documentation: Number of columns that this cell spans.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.colspan
documentation:
Sets the number of columns that this cell spans to either 1 or the
value passed in, depending on which is larger.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.rowspan
documentation: Number of rows that this cell spans.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.rowspan
documentation:
Sets the number of rows that this cell spans to either 1 or the value
passed in, depending on which is larger.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.end_row
documentation:
Returns the index of which row the cell ends at within the block. This
is changed by modifying either the starting row or the height of this
cell.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableCell.end_col
documentation:
Returns the index of which column the cell ends at within the block.
This is changed by modifying either the starting column or the width of
this cell.

A single cell in a grid table. A cell's boundaries are determined by a
starting point in the top left (start_row and start_col), as well as a
width and a height. It also has a colspan and rowspan count for cells that
span multiple rows or columns.

classes.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.add_cell
documentation:
Adds a cell to the appropriate position in the row based on where its
left and right edges are. This returns false if a cell overlaps with
another cell in the row, otherwise it returns true.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.get_all_cells
documentation:
A generator which returns all cells within the row. I use a generator
since I mainly use this in for loops, and it's more memory efficient
and code efficient with a generator.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.get_all_cells_taller_than_this_row
documentation:
A generator that gets all cells that are taller than this row (which
means they span multiple rows).

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.get_all_cells_starting_at_this_row
documentation:
A generator that gets all cells that start at this row (which means
they are not spanning from another row).

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.get_cell_starting_at_this_row_at_column
documentation:
Returns the cell (or None if no cell is found) that starts in this row,
at the column specified.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.height
documentation:
Returns the height (in number of characters) of this row. The height is
equal to the height of the shortest cell in this row.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.start_row
documentation: The index of the line in the block at which this row starts.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.end_row
documentation:
The index of the line in the block at which this row ends. This is
equal to the starting row plus the height.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.start_col
documentation:
The column in the block at which this row starts. If a cell starts at
this row, that cell's start column is returned. Otherwise, the furthest
right connected cell's (starting from the left) end column is returned.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableRow.end_col
documentation:
Returns the ending column for this row. The ending column is equal to
the last cell's ending column.

A single row in a grid table, which can contain any number of cells. Cells
within a row may not start at the same index as where the row starts, since
they may span multiple columns.

classes.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.new_row
documentation:
Creates a new row which starts at the end of the previous row. Any
cells that are larger than the height of this row are added to the new
row.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.add_cell
documentation: Adds a cell to the last row in the table.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.get_all_rows
documentation: Generator that returns all rows in the table.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.get_all_cells_starting_at_column
documentation: Generator that yields all cells in all rows that start at a specific column.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.calculate_colspans
documentation:
After all cells are added to the table, this function will calculate
all colspans for all cells in the array. It does this by walking
through all cells and finding each column in which a cell ends, and
increasing the colspans of all rows that start before that column and
ends after that column by one.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.start_row
documentation: Index of the row (in number of characters) that the table starts at.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.start_col
documentation: Index of the column (in number of characters) that the table starts at.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.width
documentation: Width (in number of characters) of the table.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.height
documentation: Height (in number of characters) of the table.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.end_row
documentation:
Returns the index of the row (in number of characters) that the table
ends at. It is equal to the starting row plus the height.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTable.end_col
documentation:
Returns the index of the column (in number of characters) that the
table ends at. It is equal to the starting row plus the height.

A grid table in its entirety.

Parameters:
    start_row: Should be 0, but can be set differently depending on the block.
    start_col: Should be 0, but can be set differently depending on the block.
    width: How many characters wide the table is.
    height: How many characters tall the table is.

classes.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor.test
documentation:
This function tests to see if the block of text passed in is a table or
not. A table is thus defined as a block of text which has more than 2
lines, has a '+-' on both the top and bottom left corners, has a '-+'
on both the top an bottom right corners, and has a '|' at the beginning
and end of the first and last rows.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor.run
documentation:
Starts parsing the block of text which contains the table. It first
finds the header (if one exists) as ended by a row of '=' characters.
It then gets all the cells in the body (as a separate table from the
header; this needs to be changed). If getting either the header or the
body fails, the table is instead rendered as a block of text.
Otherwise, it is rendered as a table with the appropriate row and
column spans.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._render_as_block
documentation: Renders a table as a block of text instead of a table.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._header_exists
documentation: Checks if a header exists. A header is defined by a row of '=' characters.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._get_header
documentation:
Separates the header of the table from the body, putting them both into
their own separate blocks and replacing the header separator with a
normal separator.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._render_rows
documentation: Renders all rows in a table into 'tr' elements, and all cells into all 'td' elements.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._get_all_cells
documentation:
Finds all cells within the block and assembles them into a table
object. Not all rows in this table will have the same length due to
the possibility that cells span multiple rows. Returns the success or
failure of finding all cells and the table object itself. If this
fails, that means that the input was malformed.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._scan_cell
documentation:
Starts scanning for a specific cell by checking the starting character
to make sure it's valid. It scans in the order right, down, left, up
to see if it can get back to its starting position. If it can, a new
GridTableCell is returned, and if it can't, None is returned.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._scan_right
documentation:
Scans right until it gets to a '+' sign. It then starts scanning down
to see if it can find a complete path, if it can't, it continues
scanning right. Otherwise, it returns the cell it found.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._scan_down
documentation:
Scans down until it gets to a '+' sign. It then starts scanning left
to see if it can find a complete path back to the starting position.
If it can, then it returns the cell it found, otherwise, it returns
None.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._scan_left
documentation:
Scans left until it gets to a '+' sign. It then starts scanning up to
verify that the path found is a complete cell and that it gets back to
the starting position. If it does, it returns the cel. Otherwise it
returns None.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._scan_up
documentation:
Scans up until it gets to a '+' sign. If the '+' sign is in the
starting position, it returns a new GridTableCell. Otherwise, it
scans right again to verify it doesn't connect to any new paths and
create a new cell. If it does, then it returns None, since its
malformed. Otherwise it continues scanning up.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._gather_text
documentation:
Gathers the text within the cell defined by the start row, start
column, end row, and end column and returns them as one string.

methods.name: markdown_grid_tables.GridTableProcessor._unindent_one_level
documentation:
Unindents the text one level, up to the index of the farthest-left
non-blank character in the text.

The markdown block processor used to parse a grid table. A malformed grid
table is generated as a block of text instead of being removed.
